title: SARS-CoV-2 Sequencing on Illumina MiSeq Using ARTIC Protocol: Part 2
author: Joel Sevinsky
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bffyjjpw
materials: [ ] Vortex and quick spin master mix.

steps.title: Dilution Plate Preparation Date/Initials:_________________ Prior to...
steps.instruction: Dilution Plate Preparation Date/Initials:_________________ Prior to starting your DNA Flex library prep, samples should be diluted into a 96 well plate as described below. Ideally, you would like each sample to be diluted such that the 30 µL final volulme of sample contains 100 ng to 500 ng of DNA. Less than 100 ng of DNA may cause the sample to be under represented in the pool, and modifications to increase the concentration of that sample are not practical when multiplexing the library prep. Much of this protocol can be achieved using 96 well plates, so having specimens organized in columns allows the use of an 8 channel multi-channel pipette.

steps.title: [ ] Label a 96 well...
steps.instruction: [ ] Label a 96 well dilution plate with the number of columns required.

steps.title: [ ] Add enough DNA to...
steps.instruction: [ ] Add enough DNA to reach a total of at least 100 ng ** and add molecular grade water (CED) to bring the total volume to 30 µL . **NOTE: Preferred amount is 100 ng to 500 ng . Less than that can lead to under representation of the sample in the final pool. More is probably ok as long as it is not extreme.

steps.title: Tagmentation Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses the...
steps.instruction: Tagmentation Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses the Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT) to tagment DNA, which is a process that fragments and tags the DNA with adapter sequences.

steps.title: [ ] Prepare tagmenation Master Mix using Calculation 1
steps.instruction: [ ] Prepare tagmenation Master Mix using Calculation 1: %s %s **NOTE: Vortex BLT prior to using. Both reagents should be at room temperature before using.

steps.title: [ ] Vortex master mix, and...
steps.instruction: [ ] Vortex master mix, and then add 20 µL to each sample well. Pipette each 10 times to mix.

steps.title: [ ] Place the plate in...
steps.instruction: [ ] Place the plate in the thermal cycler and run the TAG Program below: 55°C %s %s

steps.title: Post Tagmentation Clean-up Date/Initials:_________________ This step...
steps.instruction: Post Tagmentation Clean-up Date/Initials:_________________ This step stops the tagmentation and washes the adapter-tagged DNA on the BLT before PCR amplification. All reagents should be at Room temperature

steps.title: [ ] Check TSB for precipitates,...
steps.instruction: [ ] Check TSB for precipitates, and then add 10 µL to each tagmentation reaction (sample well). Slowly pipette to mix 10 times to resuspend the beads.

steps.title: [ ] Seal the plate with...
steps.instruction: [ ] Seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED), place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler, and run the PTC Program . %s %s

steps.title: [ ] Place plate on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear
steps.instruction: [ ] Place plate on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear.

steps.title: [ ] Remove from magnet, and add 100 µL TWB
steps.instruction: [ ] Remove from magnet, and add 100 µL TWB . Pipette to mix**. **NOTE: A deliberately slow pipetting technique minimizes the potential of TWB foaming to avoid incorrect volume aspiration and incomplete mixing.

steps.title: [ ] Place back on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear
steps.instruction: [ ] Place back on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear. Remove and discard supernatant.

steps.title: [ ] Repeat TWB washes described above 2 more times
steps.instruction: [ ] Repeat TWB washes described above 2 more times. Wait to discard supernatant and leave in magnet after second wash .

steps.title: Amplify Tagmented DNA Date/Initials:_________________ This step...
steps.instruction: Amplify Tagmented DNA Date/Initials:_________________ This step amplifies the tagmented DNA using a limited-cycle PCR program. The PCR step adds Index 1 (i7) adapters, Index 2 (i5) adapters, and sequences required for sequencing cluster generation. To confirm the indexes selected for low plexity pooling have the appropriate color balance, see the Index Adapters Pooling Guide .

steps.title: [ ] Prepare PCR master mix using Calculation 2
steps.instruction: [ ] Prepare PCR master mix using Calculation 2: %s %s

steps.title: [ ] Remove and discard TWB from samples
steps.instruction: [ ] Remove and discard TWB from samples. Remove from magnet, and immediately add 40 µL PCR master mix, pipette to mix.

steps.title: [ ] Add 10 µL indices**,...
steps.instruction: [ ] Add 10 µL indices**, and then pipette to mix. **NOTE: Use the same index strategy that you use for PusleNet organisms. If you are unfamiliar with the PulseNet protocol, see Index Adapters Pooling Guide for guidance on the indexing. A separate Appendix will be created discussing pooling best practices.

steps.title: [ ] Seal the plate with...
steps.instruction: [ ] Seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED), place on the thermal cycler, and run the BLT PCR Program on the thermal cycler. %s %s **NOTE: This is a 5 cycle PCR reaction designed for the majority of samples in 96 well plate sequencing. If your samples started with less than 100 ng of DNA, or eventually failed for low coverage in your pool, refer to page 10 of the Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Reference Guide for guidelines on how to increase the number of cycles for low input samples.

steps.title: Clean-up Libraries Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses...
steps.instruction: Clean-up Libraries Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses double-sided bead purification procedure to purify the amplified libraries.

steps.title: [ ] Centrifuge plate at 280...
steps.instruction: [ ] Centrifuge plate at 280 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute , and then place on magnet for 00:05:00 or until clear

steps.title: [ ] Transfer 45 µL of...
steps.instruction: [ ] Transfer 45 µL of supernatant to clean wells of a new midi plate (CED), and then remove from magnet

steps.title: [ ] Prepare fresh 80 %...
steps.instruction: [ ] Prepare fresh 80 % volume EtOH using the following calculation: 0.4 mL * (#samples+1) = _______ mL volume EtOH 0.1 mL * (#samples+1) = _______mL volume molecular grade water Add those two volumes together for 80 % volume EtOH.

steps.title: [ ] Seal (CED) plate and...
steps.instruction: [ ] Seal (CED) plate and incubate for at least 00:05:00 .

steps.title: [ ] Library is now ready for quantification
steps.instruction: [ ] Library is now ready for quantification. SAFE STOPPING POINT If you are stopping, seal the plate with Microseal 'B' adhesive or Microseal 'F' foil seal, and store at -25°C to -15°C for up to 30 days.

steps.title: [ ] Place plate on magnet for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: [ ] Place plate on magnet for 00:02:00 . Transfer 30 µL supernatant to new wells.

steps.title: [ ] Vortex RSB and add 32 µL
steps.instruction: [ ] Vortex RSB and add 32 µL . Pipette to mix and incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: [ ] Air dry beads approx
steps.instruction: [ ] Air dry beads approx. 00:05:00 and then remove plate from magnet

steps.title: [ ] Perform two 00:00:30 washes...
steps.instruction: [ ] Perform two 00:00:30 washes with 200 µL 80 % volume EtOH. Remove and discard supernatant after each wash. After second wash make sure to remove residual EtOH with 20 uL pipette. Perform this step on the magnetic stand without disturngin the beads.

steps.title: [ ] Place on magnet for 00:05:00 or until clear
steps.instruction: [ ] Place on magnet for 00:05:00 or until clear. Remove and discard supernatant.

steps.title: [ ] Prepare Qubit working solution using Calculation 5
steps.instruction: [ ] Prepare Qubit working solution using Calculation 5 . Label assay tubes for samples and standards. %s %s

steps.title: [ ] Combine 190 µL Qubit...
steps.instruction: [ ] Combine 190 µL Qubit working solution + 10 µL Qubit standard into labeled standard tubes

steps.title: [ ] Combine 198 µL Qubit...
steps.instruction: [ ] Combine 198 µL Qubit working solution + 2 µL extracted DNA into labeled tubes

steps.title: [ ] Vortex all tubes for...
steps.instruction: [ ] Vortex all tubes for 2-3 sec and incubate at Room temperature for minimum of 00:02:00 . Read tubes within 01:00:00

steps.title: Denaturation of Pooled Library Date/Initials:_________________ This...
steps.instruction: Denaturation of Pooled Library Date/Initials:_________________ This section demonstrates how to generate a pooled library for V3 reagents on the MiSeq.

steps.title: [ ] Centrifuge plate at 280...
steps.instruction: [ ] Centrifuge plate at 280 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

steps.title: [ ] Make 4 nanomolar (nM)...
steps.instruction: [ ] Make 4 nanomolar (nM) pool using Calculation 6 %s %s Calculation 6: 4nM Pool Pool conc. (Qubit value, ng/µl) Molarity (nM)= (Pool conc. x 1000) / 528 Volume of pool to dilute (µl)= (4 x 50) / Molarity Volume of RSB to dilute (µl)= (50 – Pool to dilute)

steps.title: [ ] Add 400 µL molecular...
steps.instruction: [ ] Add 400 µL molecular grade H 2 0 to the 100 µL 1.0 N NaOH aliquot for 0.2 N NaOH

steps.title: [ ] Combine 5 µL 0
steps.instruction: [ ] Combine 5 µL 0.2 N NaOH and 5 µL pooled DNA in a 1.5 mL tube. Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature

steps.title: [ ] Immediately add 990 µL pre-chilled HT1
steps.instruction: [ ] Immediately add 990 µL pre-chilled HT1. Pipette to mix, then use Table 2 for desired library pool concentration %s %s Desired final loading concentration □8pM □9pM □10pM □12pM □14pM □15pM □16pM □18pM □20pM Denatured Pooled Library 800µl 900µl 1000µl 600µl 700µl 750µl 800µl 900µl 1000µl HT1 200µl 100µl 0µl 400µl 300µl 250µl 200µl 100µl 0µl

steps.title: [ ] Cover and seal the...
steps.instruction: [ ] Cover and seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED).

steps.title: [ ] Add 81 µL of...
steps.instruction: [ ] Add 81 µL of SPB** to samples, pipette 10 **NOTE: Since we are using this kit on small amplicons from the ARTIC Protocol, we will follow the DNA Flex instructions for small amplicon clean up, not standard DNA input. times to mix.

description: Before You Begin This protocol is an adaptation of the Illumina Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep kit (below). It is laid out specifically for using amplicons from the ARTIC Protocol. The audience is assumed to be public health laboratorians with access to instruments and reagents used for PulseNet WGS. Four documents are linked below for reference, and can also be found following the reagent link for the Nextera DENA Flex Library Prep. The consumables and equipment document (CED) shoud be used to make sure you have the appropriate materials before beginning. There are too many consumables to list out individually in this protocol so please use the document and fill out your Lot# and Exp Dates on this document as well. The Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep kit reagents are not listed in the consumables document so they have been added to Table 1 below. Your lab will most likely have everything already for their PulseNet activities. The exception to this is PhiX which will be listed in the appropriate step. Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Reference Guide Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Checklist Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Consumables and Equipment Index Adapters Pooling Guide Nextera DNA Flex Library Prep Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20018705 %s %s
